decision: Keep release-grade live-detector-evidence gate at warning. Rebuilt Linux smoke now passes UA, UA-CH fullVersionList on SannySoft, BrowserLeaks Client Hints, Pixelscan fingerprint-check, iphey, BrowserScan, and CreepJS, webdriver, locale/timezone, screen, storage quota, hardware, WebGL configured values, and repeat-consistent sanitized Canvas 2D probe hashes, captures unscored WebAudio and FontFaceSet probes, and now records bounded BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan Permissions API plus feature-availability probes. Full headed/proxy BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan/iphey/BrowserScan/CreepJS/SannySoft matrix remains incomplete. Local macOS arm64 host headless SannySoft evidence now records a non-release warning that host Chrome still exposes HeadlessChrome; it improves macOS gap visibility but does not satisfy headed/native release coverage. macOS arm64 host headed BrowserLeaks Audio and WebGL direct evidence now extends native host drift coverage, but it is explicitly non-release because packaged macOS BrowseForge Chromium and external proxy matrix evidence remain missing. A source-level headless UA patch for embedder_support::GetUserAgentInternal is built and packaged; strict/minimal headless Docker control now emits Chrome/150.0.0.0 without the HeadlessChrome marker and clears Pixelscan botCheck, while fingerprint verdict remains inconsistent/masking. Pixelscan headed/Xvfb baseline-current, passive-native-surfaces, and minimal-native-control checks against the patched runtime also clear botCheck while remaining inconsistent/masking, so the remaining Pixelscan fingerprint finding is not limited to headless mode.
notes: macOS host BrowserLeaks WebRTC direct evidence records sanitized ICE candidate metadata without committing IP literals; packaged macOS runtime and proxy matrix evidence remain release blockers.

surfaces.evidence: internal/stealth/persona_test.go proves stable persona_id_hash/origin_salt_key for an unchanged seed and hash drift when seed changes; knowledge/manifests/local-dogfood.json records distinct profile IDs and isolated same-origin profile state for browseforge-chromium dogfood sessions.
Packaged Linux SannySoft/CDP smoke using fingerprint.user_agent config reported navigator.webdriver=false and configured Chromium UA without HeadlessChrome.

Rebuilt packaged Linux runtime passed SannySoft/CDP UA-CH smoke, BrowserLeaks Client Hints page smoke in headless and headed Docker/Xvfb modes, Pixelscan fingerprint-check UA-CH smoke in headless and headed Docker/Xvfb modes, BrowserScan UA-CH smoke in headless and headed Docker/Xvfb modes, iphey UA-CH smoke, and CreepJS UA-CH smoke in headless and headed Docker/Xvfb modes. Observed navigator.userAgentData platform=Linux, platformVersion=6.8.0, architecture=x86, bitness=64, mobile=false, model empty, wow64=false, and fullVersionList containing Chromium 150.0.7871.101.
SannySoft local dogfood rerun observed MacIntel platform on macOS host; release-grade cross-platform persona coherence remains unproven.
Linux packaged SannySoft/CDP smoke reported Intl timezone Asia/Taipei with wrapper TZ propagation plus renderer switch propagation.
Linux packaged SannySoft/CDP smoke reported zh-TW, zh;q=0.9, en;q=0.8 language list.
Packaged Linux SannySoft/CDP smoke collected by scripts/detector_harness.py reported screen 1920x1080, available screen 1920x1040, and DPR=1 from configured fingerprint profile.
Linux packaged SannySoft/CDP smoke reported hardwareConcurrency=8 and deviceMemory=8 from configured fingerprint profile.

Packaged Linux SannySoft/CDP collector rendered a deterministic 64x32 Canvas 2D probe twice from fresh container launches with fingerprint.canvas_noise=23; sanitized data_url_sha256 and image_data_sha256 matched across repeats, and no raw data URL or pixel payload is committed.

Linux packaged SannySoft/CDP smoke and BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan/iphey/BrowserScan/CreepJS page-context collectors reported the configured Intel Inc. / Intel Iris OpenGL Engine debug vendor/renderer strings. The shared collector now escapes embedded SDP newlines correctly, so passed Linux headless BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan/iphey/BrowserScan/CreepJS/SannySoft evidence records sanitized extension count, extension hash, bounded parameter hash, shader precision hash, rendered pixel hash, and pixel dimensions. detector-score-comparison.json now records a passing BrowserLeaks-vs-BrowserScan WebGL metadata cross-detector comparison and ignores warning-only non-release WebGL rows for release metadata gaps; headed/native detector confirmation remains a blocker. New macOS arm64 host headed BrowserLeaks WebGL evidence records native Apple/ANGLE vendor/renderer, extension count/hash, parameter hash, shader precision hash, rendered pixel hash, and pixel dimensions from installed system Chrome; it remains non-release artifact evidence until a signed packaged macOS BrowseForge Chromium exists.

Packaged Linux SannySoft/CDP smoke, BrowserLeaks JavaScript Web Audio page headless/headed Docker evidence, Pixelscan headed page-status hash evidence, plus BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan bounded page-context probes render deterministic OfflineAudioContext length 44100 at sampleRate 44100 and record sanitized sum/sumAbs/first-sample summaries. BrowserLeaks JavaScript exposes AudioContext fields but no numeric score; committed headless/headed field comparison passes. Rebuilt patched packaged Linux runtime CreepJS evidence from 20260710 shows freq/gain/sum/time/trap/unique all match across headless/headed Docker/Xvfb runs after dense-signal AudioBuffer noise gating. New macOS arm64 host headed BrowserLeaks JavaScript audio evidence records bounded AudioContext fields plus OfflineAudioContext sampleRate/length/sum/sumAbs summaries from installed system Chrome; it remains non-release artifact evidence until a signed packaged macOS BrowseForge Chromium exists.

Packaged Linux SannySoft/CDP smoke plus BrowserLeaks/Pixelscan/iphey/BrowserScan/CreepJS page-context collectors reported document.fonts.check availability for sampled desktop font families. BrowserLeaks Fonts headless and headed Docker/Xvfb page evidence plus CreepJS page context record bounded font metric rows plus aggregate glyph and metrics SHA-256 values; knowledge/manifests/detector-score-comparison.json reports matching BrowserLeaks headless/headed sampled font list, candidate count, glyph SHA, and metrics SHA, matching BrowserLeaks/CreepJS sampled font list/candidate count/glyph SHA with differing metric SHA, and passing Pixelscan headless-vs-headed font availability comparison. New macOS arm64 host headed BrowserLeaks Fonts evidence passed coverage and records bounded native system Chrome font metrics/glyph hashes through the BrowseForge wrapper, proving local native corpus collection works; it remains non-release artifact evidence because it used installed system Chrome rather than a signed BrowseForge macOS Chromium artifact. BrowserLeaks/CreepJS/Pixelscan native packaged platform corpus parity remains required.

CreepJS local dogfood smoke after WebRTC prefs/flag showed host connection blocked, foundation/ip unsupported/blocked, and stun connection blocked; no candidate IP literal was committed. BrowserLeaks WebRTC now has committed headless and headed Docker/Xvfb sanitized ICE candidate metadata, candidate type labels, public/private IP-literal counts, and a passing headless-vs-headed bounded comparison without committing raw addresses. External proxy/geolocation detector evidence is still required.

SannySoft local dogfood rerun reported Permissions prompt and HEADCHR_PERMISSIONS ok. The source-level feature parity patch stabilizes ContactsManager, ContactsManagerExtraProperties, ContentIndex, NetInfoDownlinkMax, and SerialPortConnected, and WebShare desktop defaults; the shared collector now records bounded Permissions API states for geolocation, notifications, camera, microphone, clipboard, midi, push, payment-handler, idle-detection, window-management, local-fonts, background-sync, persistent-storage, accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer, plus feature-availability booleans for Contacts Manager, Content Index, Network Information, storage buckets, WebBluetooth/WebHID/WebNFC/WebSerial/WebUSB, IdleDetector, Keyboard Lock, local fonts, Presentation API, VirtualKeyboard, and Window Controls Overlay, Web Share, Web Share data without requesting device access. Broader CreepJS/iphey/BrowserScan release-matrix permissions coverage remains incomplete.
Packaged Linux SannySoft/CDP smoke collected by scripts/detector_harness.py reported navigator.storage.estimate quota 8589934592 bytes for configured 8192 MiB storage quota.

Packaged Linux SannySoft/CDP, BrowserLeaks, Pixelscan, iphey, BrowserScan, and CreepJS page-context collectors reported navigator.webdriver=false; SannySoft, BrowserLeaks, Pixelscan, BrowserScan, and CreepJS have headed Docker/Xvfb evidence. CreepJS detector metrics reported 0% headless and 0% stealth with 19% like-headless in both headless and headed Docker runs. A macOS arm64 local host headless SannySoft collector recorded navigator.webdriver=false but a HeadlessChrome UA token; a new macOS arm64 local host headed SannySoft collector passed direct coverage with webdriver=false and no HeadlessChrome UA token. macOS evidence remains non-release artifact evidence until a signed BrowseForge macOS Chromium package exists. Rebuilt patched Linux runtime minimal-native-control evidence now records UA without HeadlessChrome and Pixelscan botCheck="No automated behavior detected" under strict/native headless launch; the broader fingerprint verdict remains inconsistent/masking. Pixelscan headed/Xvfb controls now show the same botCheck-clean/inconsistent split, reducing confidence that the residual Pixelscan issue is purely headless/CDP automation.

Packaged Linux BrowserLeaks Client Hints smoke executed with Chromium configured for --proxy-server=<loopback-http-proxy>; the committed local CONNECT proxy observer summary recorded BrowserLeaks HTTPS tunnel traffic through the proxy. BrowserLeaks WebRTC collection now flags sanitized candidate IP literal counts. scripts/detector_harness.py proxy-preflight now fails closed unless an external proxy URL and redacted region label are present and rejects loopback/private/local proxy authorities without emitting credentials or raw IP literals. No external proxy exit-IP/geolocation detector run has been executed.
BrowseForge dogfood used distinct profile IDs and isolated same-origin localStorage across two sessions.

Linux x64 Docker SannySoft, BrowserLeaks, Pixelscan, BrowserScan, and CreepJS smokes now exist in both headless and headed/Xvfb display modes. macOS arm64 host headed SannySoft plus BrowserLeaks Fonts, Audio, and WebGL direct evidence also exists through installed system Chrome. Windows, external proxy, and packaged native macOS/Windows detector matrix coverage remain absent.
